source: CIS GOOGLE CONTAINER OPTIMIZED OS BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure no duplicate GIDs exist

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CONTAINER OPTIMIZED OS BENCHMARK V1.2.0
input_row.title: Ensure no duplicate GIDs exist
input_row.description: Although the groupadd program will not let you create a duplicate Group ID (GID), it is possible for an administrator to manually edit the /etc/group file and change the GID field.
input_row.rationale: User groups must be assigned unique GIDs for accountability and to ensure appropriate access protections.
input_row.audit: Run the following script and verify no results are returned: #!/bin/bash cut -f3 -d":" /etc/group | sort -n | uniq -c | while read x ; do [ -z "$x" ] && break set - $x if [ $1 -gt 1 ]; then groups=$(awk -F: '($3 == n) { print $1 }' n=$2 /etc/group | xargs) echo "Duplicate GID ($2): $groups" fi done
input_row.remediation: Based on the results of the audit script, establish unique GIDs and review all files owned by the shared GID to determine which group they are supposed to belong to. Additional Information: You can also use the grpck command to check for other inconsistencies in the /etc/group file.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

This control requires verification at the OS level, more specifically within the /etc/group file on each instance of Container-Optimized OS running in GCP. The audit process involves running a shell script on each instance to detect duplicate Group IDs (GIDs), a task that mandates SSH access to the virtual machines and manual inspection of their configuration.

Given that:
- The control targets OS-level configurations not exposed via GCP APIs.
- The audit script provided is intended to be executed locally on the instances as it checks local system files.
- There are no GCP API endpoints, Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), or GCP CLI commands available to automate this check.

These factors lead to the conclusion that the compliance check for duplicate GIDs must be conducted manually, requiring remote access to each virtual machine instance running the Container-Optimized OS.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each instance running the Container-Optimized OS using the Google Cloud Console or a SSH client.
2. Execute the provided script: 
    ```bash
    #!/bin/bash
    cut -f3 -d":" /etc/group | sort -n | uniq -c | while read x ; do
      [ -z "$x" ] && break
      set - $x
      if [ $1 -gt 1 ]; then
        groups=$(awk -F: '($3 == n) { print $1 }' n=$2 /etc/group | xargs)
        echo "Duplicate GID ($2): $groups"
      fi
    done
    ```
3. Verify that no output is generated by the script, which would indicate the presence of duplicate GIDs.
4. If duplicates are found, note the groups and proceed to modify /etc/group by assigning unique GIDs to each duplicate.
5. Review all files associated with the previous GIDs to ensure they are reassigned correctly to the new GID.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CONTAINER OPTIMIZED OS BENCHMARK V1.2.0
